pli-tv-kd9:0.4.eng: 1. The account of the monk Kassapagotta

pli-tv-kd9:1.1.1.pli: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā campāyaṁ viharati gaggarāya pokkharaṇiyā tīre.
pli-tv-kd9:1.1.1.eng: At one time the Buddha was staying at Campā on the banks of the Gaggarā lotus pond.

pli-tv-kd9:1.1.2.pli: Tena kho pana samayena kāsīsu janapade vāsabhagāmo nāma hoti.
pli-tv-kd9:1.1.2.eng: At that time in the country of Kāsi there was a village called Vāsabha

pli-tv-kd9:1.1.3.pli: Tattha kassapagotto nāma bhikkhu āvāsiko hoti tantibaddho ussukkaṁ āpanno—
pli-tv-kd9:1.1.3.eng: with a resident monk called Kassapagotta. He was dedicated to the local monastery,
pli-tv-kd9:1.1.3.comm: Sp 3.380: Tantibaddhoti tasmiṁ āvāse kattabbatātantipaṭibaddho, “Tantibaddho: bound to what is to be done in regard to that monastery”.

pli-tv-kd9:1.1.4.pli: “kinti anāgatā ca pesalā bhikkhū āgaccheyyuṁ, āgatā ca pesalā bhikkhū phāsu vihareyyuṁ, ayañca āvāso vuddhiṁ viruḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjeyyā”ti.
pli-tv-kd9:1.1.4.eng: trying to get good monks to come, to help those who had come be comfortable, and to make the local monastery grow and reach maturity.

pli-tv-kd9:1.1.5.pli: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhū kāsīsu cārikaṁ caramānā yena vāsabhagāmo tadavasaruṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:1.1.5.eng: At this time a number of monks who were wandering in Kāsi arrived at Vāsabha.

pli-tv-kd9:1.1.6.pli: Addasā kho kassapagotto bhikkhu te bhikkhū dūratova āgacchante, disvāna āsanaṁ paññapesi, pādodakaṁ pādapīṭhaṁ pādakathalikaṁ upanikkhipi, paccuggantvā pattacīvaraṁ paṭiggahesi, pānīyena āpucchi, nahāne ussukkaṁ akāsi, ussukkampi akāsi yāguyā khādanīye bhattasmiṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:1.1.6.eng: When Kassapagotta saw those monks coming, he prepared seats, and he set out a foot stool, a foot scraper, and water for washing the feet. He then went out to meet them, received their bowls and robes, and asked if they wanted water to drink. He made sure they had a bath, and he helped them get congee, fresh foods, and meals.

pli-tv-kd9:1.1.7.pli: Atha kho tesaṁ āgantukānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ etadahosi—

pli-tv-kd9:1.1.8.pli: “bhaddako kho ayaṁ, āvuso, āvāsiko bhikkhu nahāne ussukkaṁ karoti, ussukkampi karoti yāguyā khādanīye bhattasmiṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:1.1.8.eng: “He’s great, this resident monk, since he helps us with all these things.

pli-tv-kd9:1.1.9.pli: Handa mayaṁ, āvuso, idheva vāsabhagāme nivāsaṁ kappemā”ti.
pli-tv-kd9:1.1.9.eng: Let’s settle down right here in Vāsabha.”

pli-tv-kd9:1.1.10.pli: Atha kho te āgantukā bhikkhū tattheva vāsabhagāme nivāsaṁ kappesuṁ.

pli-tv-kd9:1.2.2.pli: “yo kho imesaṁ āgantukānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ āgantukakilamatho so paṭippassaddho.
pli-tv-kd9:1.2.2.eng: “These monks are now rid of their tiredness from traveling.

pli-tv-kd9:1.2.3.pli: Yepime gocare appakataññuno tedānime gocare pakataññuno.
pli-tv-kd9:1.2.3.eng: And by now they know where to get alms.

pli-tv-kd9:1.2.4.pli: Dukkaraṁ kho pana parakulesu yāvajīvaṁ ussukkaṁ kātuṁ, viññatti ca manussānaṁ amanāpā.
pli-tv-kd9:1.2.4.eng: Also, in the long run it’s hard work to seek support from unrelated folk, and people don’t like to be asked.

pli-tv-kd9:1.2.5.pli: Yannūnāhaṁ na ussukkaṁ kareyyaṁ yāguyā khādanīye bhattasmin”ti.
pli-tv-kd9:1.2.5.eng: Why don’t I stop helping them get congee, fresh foods, and meals?”

pli-tv-kd9:1.2.6.pli: So na ussukkaṁ akāsi yāguyā khādanīye bhattasmiṁ.

pli-tv-kd9:1.2.7.pli: Atha kho tesaṁ āgantukānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ etadahosi—

pli-tv-kd9:1.2.8.pli: “pubbe khvāyaṁ, āvuso, āvāsiko bhikkhu nahāne ussukkaṁ akāsi, ussukkampi akāsi yāguyā khādanīye bhattasmiṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:1.2.8.eng: “Previously this resident monk made sure we got a bath, and he helped us get congee, fresh foods, and meals.

pli-tv-kd9:1.2.9.pli: Sodānāyaṁ na ussukkaṁ karoti yāguyā khādanīye bhattasmiṁ.

pli-tv-kd9:1.2.10.eng: He’s become hostile, this resident monk.

pli-tv-kd9:1.2.11.pli: Handa mayaṁ, āvuso, āvāsikaṁ bhikkhuṁ ukkhipāmā”ti.

pli-tv-kd9:1.3.1.pli: Atha kho te āgantukā bhikkhū sannipatitvā kassapagottaṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavocuṁ—
pli-tv-kd9:1.3.1.eng: Soon afterwards those newly-arrived monks gathered and confronted Kassapagotta with what had happened, adding,

pli-tv-kd9:1.3.2.pli: “pubbe kho tvaṁ, āvuso, nahāne ussukkaṁ karosi, ussukkampi karosi yāguyā khādanīye bhattasmiṁ.

pli-tv-kd9:1.3.3.pli: Sodāni tvaṁ na ussukkaṁ karosi yāguyā khādanīye bhattasmiṁ.

pli-tv-kd9:1.3.6.eng: “No. I haven’t committed any offense that I should recognize.”

pli-tv-kd9:1.3.7.pli: Atha kho te āgantukā bhikkhū kassapagottaṁ bhikkhuṁ āpattiyā adassane ukkhipiṁsu.
pli-tv-kd9:1.3.7.eng: Those newly-arrived monks then ejected Kassapagotta for not recognizing an offense.

pli-tv-kd9:1.3.9.pli: “ahaṁ kho etaṁ na jānāmi ‘āpatti vā esā anāpatti vā, āpanno camhi anāpanno vā, ukkhitto camhi anukkhitto vā, dhammikena vā adhammikena vā, kuppena vā akuppena vā, ṭhānārahena vā aṭṭhānārahena vā’.
pli-tv-kd9:1.3.9.eng: “I don’t actually know whether this was an offense or not, whether I’ve committed one or not, whether I’ve been ejected or not, whether it was legitimate or not, whether it’s reversible or not, whether it’s fit to stand or not.

pli-tv-kd9:1.3.10.pli: Yannūnāhaṁ campaṁ gantvā bhagavantaṁ etamatthaṁ puccheyyan”ti.
pli-tv-kd9:1.3.10.eng: Let me go to Campā and ask the Buddha.”

pli-tv-kd9:1.4.1.pli: Atha kho kassapagotto bhikkhu senāsanaṁ saṁsāmetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena campā tena pakkāmi.
pli-tv-kd9:1.4.1.eng: He then put his dwelling in order, took his bowl and robe, and set out for Campā.

pli-tv-kd9:1.4.2.pli: Anupubbena yena campā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami, upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.
pli-tv-kd9:1.4.2.eng: When he eventually arrived, he went to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down.

pli-tv-kd9:1.4.3.pli: Āciṇṇaṁ kho panetaṁ buddhānaṁ bhagavantānaṁ āgantukehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ paṭisammodituṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:1.4.3.eng: Since it is the custom for Buddhas to greet newly-arrived monks,

pli-tv-kd9:1.4.4.pli: Atha kho bhagavā kassapagottaṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca—

pli-tv-kd9:1.4.5.eng: “I hope you’re keeping well, monk, I hope you’re getting by?

pli-tv-kd9:1.4.6.eng: I hope you’re not tired from traveling?

pli-tv-kd9:1.4.7.eng: And where have you come from?”

pli-tv-kd9:1.4.8.eng: “I’m keeping well, sir, I’m getting by.

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.1.pli: Atthi, bhante, kāsīsu janapade vāsabhagāmo nāma.
pli-tv-kd9:1.5.1.eng: And he told the Buddha all that had happened, adding,

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.2.pli: Tatthāhaṁ, bhagavā, āvāsiko tantibaddho ussukkaṁ āpanno—

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.3.pli: ‘kinti anāgatā ca pesalā bhikkhū āgaccheyyuṁ, āgatā ca pesalā bhikkhū phāsu vihareyyuṁ, ayañca āvāso vuddhiṁ viruḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjeyyā’ti.

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.4.pli: Atha kho, bhante, sambahulā bhikkhū kāsīsu cārikaṁ caramānā yena vāsabhagāmo tadavasaruṁ.

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.5.pli: Addasaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, te bhikkhū dūratova āgacchante, disvāna āsanaṁ paññapesiṁ, pādodakaṁ pādapīṭhaṁ pādakathalikaṁ upanikkhipiṁ, paccuggantvā pattacīvaraṁ paṭiggahesiṁ, pānīyena apucchiṁ, nahāne ussukkaṁ akāsiṁ, ussukkampi akāsiṁ yāguyā khādanīye bhattasmiṁ.

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.6.pli: Atha kho tesaṁ, bhante, āgantukānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ etadahosi—

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.7.pli: ‘bhaddako kho ayaṁ āvuso āvāsiko bhikkhu nahāne ussukkaṁ karoti, ussukkampi karoti yāguyā khādanīye bhattasmiṁ.

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.8.pli: Handa mayaṁ, āvuso, idheva vāsabhagāme nivāsaṁ kappemā’ti.

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.9.pli: Atha kho te, bhante, āgantukā bhikkhū tattheva vāsabhagāme nivāsaṁ kappesuṁ.

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.11.pli: ‘yo kho imesaṁ āgantukānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ āgantukakilamatho so paṭippassaddho.

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.12.pli: Yepime gocare appakataññuno tedānime gocare pakataññuno.

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.13.pli: Dukkaraṁ kho pana parakulesu yāvajīvaṁ ussukkaṁ kātuṁ, viññatti ca manussānaṁ amanāpā.

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.14.pli: Yannūnāhaṁ na ussukkaṁ kareyyaṁ yāguyā khādanīye bhattasmin’ti.

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.15.pli: So kho ahaṁ, bhante, na ussukkaṁ akāsiṁ yāguyā khādanīye bhattasmiṁ.

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.16.pli: Atha kho tesaṁ, bhante, āgantukānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ etadahosi—

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.17.pli: ‘pubbe khvāyaṁ, āvuso, āvāsiko bhikkhu nahāne ussukkaṁ karoti, ussukkampi karoti yāguyā khādanīye bhattasmiṁ.

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.18.pli: Sodānāyaṁ na ussukkaṁ karoti yāguyā khādanīye bhattasmiṁ.

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.20.pli: Handa mayaṁ, āvuso, āvāsikaṁ bhikkhuṁ ukkhipāmā’ti.

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.21.pli: Atha kho te, bhante, āgantukā bhikkhū sannipatitvā maṁ etadavocuṁ—

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.22.pli: ‘pubbe kho tvaṁ, āvuso, nahāne ussukkaṁ karosi, ussukkampi karosi yāguyā khādanīye bhattasmiṁ.

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.23.pli: Sodāni tvaṁ na ussukkaṁ karosi yāguyā khādanīye bhattasmiṁ.

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.27.pli: Atha kho te, bhante, āgantukā bhikkhū maṁ āpattiyā adassane ukkhipiṁsu.

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.29.pli: ‘ahaṁ kho etaṁ na jānāmi “āpatti vā esā anāpatti vā, āpanno camhi anāpanno vā, ukkhitto camhi anukkhitto vā, dhammikena vā adhammikena vā, kuppena vā akuppena vā, ṭhānārahena vā aṭṭhānārahena vā”.

pli-tv-kd9:1.5.30.pli: Yannūnāhaṁ campaṁ gantvā bhagavantaṁ etamatthaṁ puccheyyan’ti.

pli-tv-kd9:1.6.4.eng: You’ve been ejected by an illegitimate legal procedure that’s reversible and unfit to stand.

pli-tv-kd9:1.6.5.pli: Gaccha tvaṁ, bhikkhu, tattheva vāsabhagāme nivāsaṁ kappehī”ti.
pli-tv-kd9:1.6.5.eng: Go back and stay right there in the village of Vāsabha.”

pli-tv-kd9:1.6.6.pli: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho kassapagotto bhikkhu bhagavato paṭissuṇitvā uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā yena vāsabhagāmo tena pakkāmi.
pli-tv-kd9:1.6.6.eng: “Yes, sir.” He got up from his seat, bowed down, circumambulated the Buddha with his right side toward him, and set out for Vāsabha.

pli-tv-kd9:1.7.1.pli: Atha kho tesaṁ āgantukānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ ahudeva kukkuccaṁ, ahu vippaṭisāro—
pli-tv-kd9:1.7.1.eng: Soon those newly-arrived monks became anxious and remorseful:

pli-tv-kd9:1.7.2.pli: “alābhā vata no, na vata no lābhā;

pli-tv-kd9:1.7.3.pli: dulladdhaṁ vata no, na vata no suladdhaṁ, ye mayaṁ suddhaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpattikaṁ avatthusmiṁ akāraṇe ukkhipimhā.
pli-tv-kd9:1.7.3.eng: that we have ejected, without reason, a pure monk who hadn’t committed any offense.

pli-tv-kd9:1.7.4.pli: Handa mayaṁ, āvuso, campaṁ gantvā bhagavato santike accayaṁ accayato desemā”ti.
pli-tv-kd9:1.7.4.eng: Well then, let’s go to Campā and confess our mistake to the Buddha.”

pli-tv-kd9:1.7.5.pli: Atha kho te āgantukā bhikkhū senāsanaṁ saṁsāmetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena campā tena pakkamiṁsu.
pli-tv-kd9:1.7.5.eng: They then put their dwellings in order, took their bowls and robes, and set out for Campā.

pli-tv-kd9:1.7.6.pli: Anupubbena yena campā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu, upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu.
pli-tv-kd9:1.7.6.eng: When they eventually arrived, they went to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down.

pli-tv-kd9:1.7.7.pli: Āciṇṇaṁ kho panetaṁ buddhānaṁ bhagavantānaṁ āgantukehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ paṭisammodituṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:1.7.7.eng: Since it is the custom for Buddhas to greet newly-arrived monks,

pli-tv-kd9:1.7.8.pli: Atha kho bhagavā te bhikkhū etadavoca—

pli-tv-kd9:1.7.9.eng: “I hope you’re keeping well, monks, I hope you’re getting by?

pli-tv-kd9:1.7.10.eng: I hope you’re not tired from traveling?

pli-tv-kd9:1.7.11.eng: And where have you come from?”

pli-tv-kd9:1.7.12.eng: “We’re keeping well, sir, we’re getting by.

pli-tv-kd9:1.7.13.pli: appakilamathena ca mayaṁ, bhante, addhānaṁ āgatā.

pli-tv-kd9:1.7.14.pli: Atthi, bhante, kāsīsu janapade vāsabhagāmo nāma.
pli-tv-kd9:1.7.14.eng: There’s a village in the country of Kāsi called Vāsabha.

pli-tv-kd9:1.8.1.eng: “Are you the ones who ejected the resident monk?”

pli-tv-kd9:1.8.6.pli: “ananucchavikaṁ, moghapurisā, ananulomikaṁ appatirūpaṁ assāmaṇakaṁ akappiyaṁ akaraṇīyaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:1.8.6.eng: “Foolish men, it’s not suitable, it’s not proper, it’s not worthy of a monastic, it’s not allowable, it’s not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:1.8.7.pli: Kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, suddhaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpattikaṁ avatthusmiṁ akāraṇe ukkhipissatha.
pli-tv-kd9:1.8.7.eng: How could you, without reason, eject a pure monk who hadn’t committed any offense?

pli-tv-kd9:1.8.9.pli: vigarahitvā dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
pli-tv-kd9:1.8.9.eng: After rebuking them … he gave a teaching and addressed the monks:

pli-tv-kd9:1.8.10.pli: “na, bhikkhave, suddho bhikkhu anāpattiko avatthusmiṁ akāraṇe ukkhipitabbo.
pli-tv-kd9:1.8.10.eng: “You shouldn’t, without reason, eject a pure monk who hasn’t committed any offense.

pli-tv-kd9:1.8.11.eng: If you do, you commit an offense of wrong conduct.”

pli-tv-kd9:1.9.1.pli: Atha kho te bhikkhū uṭṭhāyāsanā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā bhagavato pādesu sirasā nipatitvā bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ—
pli-tv-kd9:1.9.1.eng: Those monks then got up from their seats, arranged their upper robes over one shoulder, bowed down with their heads at the Buddha’s feet, and said,

pli-tv-kd9:1.9.2.pli: “accayo no, bhante, accagamā yathābāle yathāmūḷhe yathāakusale, ye mayaṁ suddhaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpattikaṁ avatthusmiṁ akāraṇe ukkhipimhā.
pli-tv-kd9:1.9.2.eng: “Sir, we have made a mistake. We’ve been foolish, confused, and unskillful in ejecting, without reason, a pure monk who hadn’t committed any offense.

pli-tv-kd9:1.9.3.pli: Tesaṁ no, bhante, bhagavā accayaṁ accayato paṭiggaṇhātu āyatiṁ saṁvarāyā”ti.
pli-tv-kd9:1.9.3.eng: Please accept our confession so that we may restrain ourselves in the future.”

pli-tv-kd9:1.9.4.pli: “Taggha tumhe, bhikkhave, accayo accagamā yathābāle yathāmūḷhe yathāakusale, ye tumhe suddhaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpattikaṁ avatthusmiṁ akāraṇe ukkhipittha.
pli-tv-kd9:1.9.4.eng: “You have certainly made a mistake. You’ve been foolish, confused, and unskillful.

pli-tv-kd9:1.9.5.pli: Yato ca kho tumhe, bhikkhave, accayaṁ accayato disvā yathādhammaṁ paṭikarotha, taṁ vo mayaṁ paṭiggaṇhāma.
pli-tv-kd9:1.9.5.eng: But since you acknowledge your mistake and make proper amends, I forgive you.

pli-tv-kd9:1.9.6.pli: Vuddhihesā, bhikkhave, ariyassa vinaye yo accayaṁ accayato disvā yathādhammaṁ paṭikaroti, āyatiṁ saṁvaraṁ āpajjatī”ti.
pli-tv-kd9:1.9.6.eng: For this is called growth in the training of the noble ones: acknowledging a mistake, making proper amends, and undertaking restraint for the future.”

pli-tv-kd9:2.1.0.eng: 2. Discussion of illegitimate legal procedures done by an incomplete assembly, etc.

pli-tv-kd9:2.1.1.pli: Tena kho pana samayena campāyaṁ bhikkhū evarūpāni kammāni karonti—
pli-tv-kd9:2.1.1.eng: At that time the monks at Campā did legal procedures such as these:

pli-tv-kd9:2.1.2.pli: adhammena vaggakammaṁ karonti, adhammena samaggakammaṁ karonti;
pli-tv-kd9:2.1.2.eng: illegitimate legal procedures done by an incomplete assembly, illegitimate legal procedures done by a unanimous assembly,

pli-tv-kd9:2.1.3.pli: dhammena vaggakammaṁ karonti, dhammapatirūpakena vaggakammaṁ karonti;
pli-tv-kd9:2.1.3.eng: legitimate legal procedures done by an incomplete assembly, legitimate-like legal procedures done by an incomplete assembly,

pli-tv-kd9:2.1.4.eng: legitimate-like legal procedures done by a unanimous assembly,

pli-tv-kd9:2.1.5.pli: ekopi ekaṁ ukkhipati, ekopi dve ukkhipati, ekopi sambahule ukkhipati, ekopi saṅghaṁ ukkhipati;
pli-tv-kd9:2.1.5.eng: one person ejecting another, one ejecting two, one ejecting three, one ejecting a sangha,
pli-tv-kd9:2.1.5.comm: “Three” renders sambahula. See Appendix of Technical Terms.

pli-tv-kd9:2.1.6.pli: dvepi ekaṁ ukkhipanti, dvepi dve ukkhipanti, dvepi sambahule ukkhipanti, dvepi saṅghaṁ ukkhipanti;
pli-tv-kd9:2.1.6.eng: two ejecting one, two ejecting two, two ejecting three, two ejecting a sangha,

pli-tv-kd9:2.1.8.pli: sambahulāpi dve ukkhipanti, sambahulāpi sambahule ukkhipanti, sambahulāpi saṅghaṁ ukkhipanti;
pli-tv-kd9:2.1.8.eng: three ejecting two, three ejecting three, three ejecting a sangha,

pli-tv-kd9:2.2.1.pli: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
pli-tv-kd9:2.2.1.eng: The monks of few desires complained and criticized them,

pli-tv-kd9:2.2.2.pli: “kathañhi nāma campāyaṁ bhikkhū evarūpāni kammāni karissanti—
pli-tv-kd9:2.2.2.eng: “How can the monks at Campā do such legal procedures?”

pli-tv-kd9:2.2.3.pli: adhammena vaggakammaṁ karissanti, adhammena samaggakammaṁ karissanti, dhammena vaggakammaṁ karissanti, dhammapatirūpakena vaggakammaṁ karissanti, dhammapatirūpakena samaggakammaṁ karissanti, ekopi ekaṁ ukkhipissati, ekopi dve ukkhipissati, ekopi sambahule ukkhipissati, ekopi saṅghaṁ ukkhipissati, dvepi ekaṁ ukkhipissanti, dvepi dve ukkhipissanti, dvepi sambahule ukkhipissanti, dvepi saṅghaṁ ukkhipissanti, sambahulāpi ekaṁ ukkhipissanti, sambahulāpi dve ukkhipissanti, sambahulāpi sambahule ukkhipissanti, sambahulāpi saṅghaṁ ukkhipissanti, saṅghopi saṅghaṁ ukkhipissatī”ti.

pli-tv-kd9:2.2.4.pli: Atha kho te bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe…

pli-tv-kd9:2.2.5.pli: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, campāyaṁ bhikkhū evarūpāni kammāni karonti—
pli-tv-kd9:2.2.5.eng: “Is it true, monks, that the monks at Campā do this?”

pli-tv-kd9:2.2.10.pli: “ananucchavikaṁ, bhikkhave, tesaṁ moghapurisānaṁ ananulomikaṁ appatirūpaṁ assāmaṇakaṁ akappiyaṁ akaraṇīyaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:2.2.10.eng: “It’s not suitable for those foolish men, it’s not proper, it’s not worthy of a monastic, it’s not allowable, it’s not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:2.2.11.pli: Kathañhi nāma te, bhikkhave, moghapurisā evarūpāni kammāni karissanti—
pli-tv-kd9:2.2.11.eng: How can they do such legal procedures?

pli-tv-kd9:2.2.16.eng: he gave a teaching and addressed the monks:

pli-tv-kd9:2.3.1.pli: “Adhammena ce, bhikkhave, vaggakammaṁ akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ,
pli-tv-kd9:2.3.1.eng: “Illegitimate legal procedures done by an incomplete assembly are invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:2.3.2.pli: adhammena samaggakammaṁ akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ,
pli-tv-kd9:2.3.2.eng: Illegitimate legal procedures done by a unanimous assembly are invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:2.3.3.pli: dhammena vaggakammaṁ akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ;
pli-tv-kd9:2.3.3.eng: Legitimate legal procedures done by an incomplete assembly are invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:2.3.4.pli: dhammapatirūpakena vaggakammaṁ akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ,
pli-tv-kd9:2.3.4.eng: Legitimate-like legal procedures done by an incomplete assembly are invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:2.3.5.pli: dhammapatirūpakena samaggakammaṁ akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ;
pli-tv-kd9:2.3.5.eng: Legitimate-like legal procedures done by a unanimous assembly are invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:2.3.6.pli: ekopi ekaṁ ukkhipati akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ,
pli-tv-kd9:2.3.6.eng: One person ejecting another is invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:2.3.7.pli: ekopi dve ukkhipati akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ,
pli-tv-kd9:2.3.7.eng: One ejecting two is invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:2.3.8.pli: ekopi sambahule ukkhipati akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ,
pli-tv-kd9:2.3.8.eng: One ejecting three is invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:2.3.9.pli: ekopi saṅghaṁ ukkhipati akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ;,
pli-tv-kd9:2.3.9.eng: One ejecting a sangha is invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:2.3.10.pli: dvepi ekaṁ ukkhipanti akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ,
pli-tv-kd9:2.3.10.eng: Two ejecting one is invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:2.3.11.pli: dvepi dve ukkhipanti akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ,
pli-tv-kd9:2.3.11.eng: Two ejecting two is invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:2.3.12.pli: dvepi sambahule ukkhipanti akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ,
pli-tv-kd9:2.3.12.eng: Two ejecting three is invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:2.3.13.pli: dvepi saṅghaṁ ukkhipanti akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ;,
pli-tv-kd9:2.3.13.eng: Two ejecting a sangha is invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:2.3.14.pli: sambahulāpi ekaṁ ukkhipanti akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ,
pli-tv-kd9:2.3.14.eng: Three ejecting one is invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:2.3.15.pli: sambahulāpi dve ukkhipanti akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ,
pli-tv-kd9:2.3.15.eng: Three ejecting two is invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:2.3.16.pli: sambahulāpi sambahule ukkhipanti akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ,
pli-tv-kd9:2.3.16.eng: Three ejecting three is invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:2.3.17.pli: sambahulāpi saṅghaṁ ukkhipanti akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ;,
pli-tv-kd9:2.3.17.eng: Three ejecting a sangha is invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:2.3.18.pli: saṅghopi saṅghaṁ ukkhipati akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:2.3.18.eng: A sangha ejecting a sangha is invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:2.4.1.eng: There are four kinds of legal procedures:

pli-tv-kd9:2.4.2.pli: adhammena vaggakammaṁ, adhammena samaggakammaṁ, dhammena vaggakammaṁ, dhammena samaggakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:2.4.2.eng: an illegitimate legal procedure done by an incomplete assembly, an illegitimate legal procedure done by a unanimous assembly, a legitimate legal procedure done by an incomplete assembly, and a legitimate legal procedure done by a unanimous assembly.

pli-tv-kd9:2.4.3.pli: Tatra, bhikkhave, yadidaṁ adhammena vaggakammaṁ, idaṁ, bhikkhave, kammaṁ adhammattā vaggattā kuppaṁ aṭṭhānārahaṁ;
pli-tv-kd9:2.4.3.eng: The illegitimate legal procedure done by an incomplete assembly is reversible and unfit to stand, because it’s illegitimate and the assembly is incomplete.

pli-tv-kd9:2.4.4.pli: na, bhikkhave, evarūpaṁ kammaṁ kātabbaṁ, na ca mayā evarūpaṁ kammaṁ anuññātaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:2.4.4.eng: You shouldn’t do such procedures. I haven’t allowed such procedures.

pli-tv-kd9:2.4.5.pli: Tatra, bhikkhave, yadidaṁ adhammena samaggakammaṁ, idaṁ, bhikkhave, kammaṁ adhammattā kuppaṁ aṭṭhānārahaṁ;
pli-tv-kd9:2.4.5.eng: The illegitimate legal procedure done by a unanimous assembly is reversible and unfit to stand, because it’s illegitimate.

pli-tv-kd9:2.4.6.pli: na, bhikkhave, evarūpaṁ kammaṁ kātabbaṁ, na ca mayā evarūpaṁ kammaṁ anuññātaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:2.4.6.eng: You shouldn’t do such procedures. I haven’t allowed such procedures.

pli-tv-kd9:2.4.7.pli: Tatra, bhikkhave, yadidaṁ dhammena vaggakammaṁ, idaṁ, bhikkhave, kammaṁ vaggattā kuppaṁ aṭṭhānārahaṁ;
pli-tv-kd9:2.4.7.eng: The legitimate legal procedure done by an incomplete assembly is reversible and unfit to stand, because the assembly is incomplete.

pli-tv-kd9:2.4.8.pli: na, bhikkhave, evarūpaṁ kammaṁ kātabbaṁ, na ca mayā evarūpaṁ kammaṁ anuññātaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:2.4.8.eng: You shouldn’t do such procedures. I haven’t allowed such procedures.

pli-tv-kd9:2.4.9.pli: Tatra, bhikkhave, yadidaṁ dhammena samaggakammaṁ, idaṁ, bhikkhave, kammaṁ dhammattā samaggattā akuppaṁ ṭhānārahaṁ;
pli-tv-kd9:2.4.9.eng: The legitimate legal procedure done by a unanimous assembly is irreversible and fit to stand, because it’s legitimate and the assembly is unanimous.

pli-tv-kd9:2.4.10.pli: evarūpaṁ, bhikkhave, kammaṁ kātabbaṁ, evarūpañca mayā kammaṁ anuññātaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:2.4.10.eng: You should do such procedures. I have allowed such procedures.

pli-tv-kd9:2.4.11.pli: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, evarūpaṁ kammaṁ karissāma yadidaṁ dhammena samagganti—
pli-tv-kd9:2.4.11.eng: And so, monks, you should train yourselves like this: ‘We will perform legitimate legal procedures done by a unanimous assembly.’”

pli-tv-kd9:3.1.0.eng: 3. Discussion of legal procedures deficient in motion, etc.

pli-tv-kd9:3.1.1.pli: Tena kho pana samayena chabbaggiyā bhikkhū evarūpāni kammāni karonti—
pli-tv-kd9:3.1.1.eng: At that time the monks from the group of six did legal procedures such as these:

pli-tv-kd9:3.1.2.pli: adhammena vaggakammaṁ karonti, adhammena samaggakammaṁ karonti;
pli-tv-kd9:3.1.2.eng: illegitimate procedures done by an incomplete assembly; illegitimate procedures done by a unanimous assembly;

pli-tv-kd9:3.1.3.pli: dhammena vaggakammaṁ karonti, dhammapatirūpakena vaggakammaṁ karonti, dhammapatirūpakena samaggakammaṁ karonti;
pli-tv-kd9:3.1.3.eng: legitimate procedures done by an incomplete assembly; legitimate-like procedures done by an incomplete assembly; legitimate-like procedures done by a unanimous assembly;

pli-tv-kd9:3.1.4.pli: ñattivipannampi kammaṁ karonti anussāvanasampannaṁ, anussāvanavipannampi kammaṁ karonti ñattisampannaṁ, ñattivipannampi anussāvanavipannampi kammaṁ karonti;
pli-tv-kd9:3.1.4.eng: procedures deficient in motion but complete in announcement; procedures deficient in announcement but complete in motion; procedures deficient in both motion and announcement;
pli-tv-kd9:3.1.4.comm: “Announcement” renders anussāvana. When used to describe elements of a saṅghakamma, “a legal procedure”, anussāvana and kammavācā are used synonymously. In these cases they refer to the one or three “announcements” that follow the motion, and so I render them both as “announcement”. Occasionally, however, anussāvana, but not kammavācā, is used to describe the full legal procedure of both motion and announcements. In such instances I render it as “proclamation”.

pli-tv-kd9:3.1.5.pli: aññatrāpi dhammā kammaṁ karonti, aññatrāpi vinayā kammaṁ karonti, aññatrāpi satthusāsanā kammaṁ karonti;
pli-tv-kd9:3.1.5.eng: procedures not done according to the Teaching; procedures not done according to the Monastic Law; procedures not done according to the Teacher’s instructions;

pli-tv-kd9:3.1.6.pli: paṭikuṭṭhakatampi kammaṁ karonti adhammikaṁ kuppaṁ aṭṭhānārahaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.1.6.eng: procedures that had been objected to, that were illegitimate, reversible, and unfit to stand.

pli-tv-kd9:3.1.7.pli: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
pli-tv-kd9:3.1.7.eng: The monks of few desires complained and criticized them,

pli-tv-kd9:3.1.8.pli: “kathañhi nāma chabbaggiyā bhikkhū evarūpāni kammāni karissanti—
pli-tv-kd9:3.1.8.eng: “How can the monks from the group of six do such legal procedures?”

pli-tv-kd9:3.1.9.pli: adhammena vaggakammaṁ karissanti, adhammena samaggakammaṁ karissanti;

pli-tv-kd9:3.1.10.pli: dhammena vaggakammaṁ karissanti, dhammapatirūpakena vaggakammaṁ karissanti, dhammapatirūpakena samaggakammaṁ karissanti;

pli-tv-kd9:3.1.11.pli: ñattivipannampi kammaṁ karissanti anussāvanasampannaṁ, anussāvanavipannampi kammaṁ karissanti ñattisampannaṁ, ñattivipannampi anussāvanavipannampi kammaṁ karissanti;

pli-tv-kd9:3.1.12.pli: aññatrāpi dhammā kammaṁ karissanti, aññatrāpi vinayā kammaṁ karissanti, aññatrāpi satthusāsanā kammaṁ karissanti;

pli-tv-kd9:3.1.13.pli: paṭikuṭṭhakatampi kammaṁ karissanti adhammikaṁ kuppaṁ aṭṭhānārahan”ti.

pli-tv-kd9:3.1.14.pli: Atha kho te bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe…

pli-tv-kd9:3.1.15.pli: “saccaṁ kira, bhikkhave, chabbaggiyā bhikkhū evarūpāni kammāni karonti—
pli-tv-kd9:3.1.15.eng: “Is it true, monks, that the monks from the group of six do this?”

pli-tv-kd9:3.1.17.pli: paṭikuṭṭhakatampi kammaṁ karonti adhammikaṁ kuppaṁ aṭṭhānārahan”ti?

pli-tv-kd9:3.1.20.pli: vigarahitvā dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
pli-tv-kd9:3.1.20.eng: He then gave a teaching and addressed the monks:

pli-tv-kd9:3.2.1.pli: “Adhammena ce, bhikkhave, vaggakammaṁ akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ;
pli-tv-kd9:3.2.1.eng: “Illegitimate legal procedures done by an incomplete assembly are invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:3.2.2.pli: adhammena samaggakammaṁ akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ;
pli-tv-kd9:3.2.2.eng: Illegitimate legal procedures done by a unanimous assembly are invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:3.2.3.pli: dhammena vaggakammaṁ akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ;
pli-tv-kd9:3.2.3.eng: Legitimate legal procedures done by an incomplete assembly are invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:3.2.4.pli: dhammapatirūpakena vaggakammaṁ akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ;
pli-tv-kd9:3.2.4.eng: Legitimate-like legal procedures done by an incomplete assembly are invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:3.2.5.pli: dhammapatirūpakena samaggakammaṁ akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.2.5.eng: Legitimate-like legal procedures done by a unanimous assembly are invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:3.2.6.pli: Ñattivipannañce, bhikkhave, kammaṁ anussāvanasampannaṁ akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ;
pli-tv-kd9:3.2.6.eng: Legal procedures deficient in motion but complete in announcement are invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:3.2.7.pli: anussāvanavipannañce, bhikkhave, kammaṁ ñattisampannaṁ akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ;
pli-tv-kd9:3.2.7.eng: Legal procedures deficient in announcement but complete in motion are invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:3.2.8.pli: ñattivipannañce, bhikkhave, kammaṁ anussāvanavipannaṁ akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ;
pli-tv-kd9:3.2.8.eng: Legal procedures deficient in both motion and announcement are invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:3.2.9.pli: aññatrāpi dhammā kammaṁ akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ;
pli-tv-kd9:3.2.9.eng: Legal procedures not done according to the Teaching are invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:3.2.10.pli: aññatrāpi vinayā kammaṁ akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ;
pli-tv-kd9:3.2.10.eng: Legal procedures not done according to the Monastic Law are invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:3.2.11.pli: aññatrāpi satthusāsanā kammaṁ akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ;
pli-tv-kd9:3.2.11.eng: Legal procedures not done according to the Teacher’s instructions are invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:3.2.12.pli: paṭikuṭṭhakatañce, bhikkhave, kammaṁ adhammikaṁ kuppaṁ aṭṭhānārahaṁ akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.2.12.eng: Legal procedures that have been objected to, that are illegitimate, reversible, and unfit to stand are invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:3.3.1.eng: And, monks, there are six kinds of legal procedures:

pli-tv-kd9:3.3.2.pli: adhammakammaṁ, vaggakammaṁ, samaggakammaṁ, dhammapatirūpakena vaggakammaṁ, dhammapatirūpakena samaggakammaṁ, dhammena samaggakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.3.2.eng: illegitimate legal procedures, legal procedures done by an incomplete assembly, legal procedures done by a unanimous assembly, legitimate-like legal procedures done by an incomplete assembly, legitimate-like legal procedures done by a unanimous assembly, legitimate legal procedures done by a unanimous assembly.

pli-tv-kd9:3.3.4.pli: Ñattidutiye ce, bhikkhave, kamme ekāya ñattiyā kammaṁ karoti, na ca kammavācaṁ anussāveti—adhammakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.3.4.eng: If a procedure requires one motion and one announcement, but they do it with one motion and no announcement, it’s an illegitimate legal procedure.

pli-tv-kd9:3.3.5.pli: Ñattidutiye ce, bhikkhave, kamme dvīhi ñattīhi kammaṁ karoti, na ca kammavācaṁ anussāveti—adhammakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.3.5.eng: If a procedure requires one motion and one announcement, but they do it with two motions and no announcement, it’s an illegitimate legal procedure.

pli-tv-kd9:3.3.6.pli: Ñattidutiye ce, bhikkhave, kamme ekāya kammavācāya kammaṁ karoti, na ca ñattiṁ ṭhapeti—adhammakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.3.6.eng: If a procedure requires one motion and one announcement, but they do it with one announcement and no motion, it’s an illegitimate legal procedure.

pli-tv-kd9:3.3.7.pli: Ñattidutiye ce, bhikkhave, kamme dvīhi kammavācāhi kammaṁ karoti, na ca ñattiṁ ṭhapeti—adhammakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.3.7.eng: If a procedure requires one motion and one announcement, but they do it with two announcements and no motion, it’s an illegitimate legal procedure.

pli-tv-kd9:3.4.1.pli: Ñatticatutthe ce, bhikkhave, kamme ekāya ñattiyā kammaṁ karoti, na ca kammavācaṁ anussāveti—
pli-tv-kd9:3.4.1.eng: If a procedure requires one motion and three announcements, but they do it with one motion and no announcement,

pli-tv-kd9:3.4.2.eng: it is an illegitimate legal procedure.

pli-tv-kd9:3.4.3.pli: Ñatticatutthe ce, bhikkhave, kamme dvīhi ñattīhi kammaṁ karoti, na ca kammavācaṁ anussāveti—adhammakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.4.3.eng: If a procedure requires one motion and three announcements, but they do it with two motions and no announcement, it’s an illegitimate legal procedure.

pli-tv-kd9:3.4.4.pli: Ñatticatutthe ce, bhikkhave, kamme tīhi ñattīhi kammaṁ karoti, na ca kammavācaṁ anussāveti—adhammakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.4.4.eng: If a procedure requires one motion and three announcements, but they do it with three motions and no announcement, it’s an illegitimate legal procedure.

pli-tv-kd9:3.4.5.pli: Ñatticatutthe ce, bhikkhave, kamme catūhi ñattīhi kammaṁ karoti, na ca kammavācaṁ anussāveti—adhammakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.4.5.eng: If a procedure requires one motion and three announcements, but they do it with four motions and no announcement, it’s an illegitimate legal procedure.

pli-tv-kd9:3.4.6.pli: Ñatticatutthe ce, bhikkhave, kamme ekāya kammavācāya kammaṁ karoti, na ca ñattiṁ ṭhapeti—adhammakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.4.6.eng: If a procedure requires one motion and three announcements, but they do it with one announcement and no motion, it’s an illegitimate legal procedure.

pli-tv-kd9:3.4.7.pli: Ñatticatutthe ce, bhikkhave, kamme dvīhi kammavācāhi kammaṁ karoti, na ca ñattiṁ ṭhapeti—adhammakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.4.7.eng: If a procedure requires one motion and three announcements, but they do it with two announcements and no motion, it’s an illegitimate legal procedure.

pli-tv-kd9:3.4.8.pli: Ñatticatutthe ce, bhikkhave, kamme tīhi kammavācāhi kammaṁ karoti, na ca ñattiṁ ṭhapeti—adhammakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.4.8.eng: If a procedure requires one motion and three announcements, but they do it with three announcements and no motion, it’s an illegitimate legal procedure.

pli-tv-kd9:3.4.9.pli: Ñatticatutthe ce, bhikkhave, kamme catūhi kammavācāhi kammaṁ karoti, na ca ñattiṁ ṭhapeti—adhammakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.4.9.eng: If a procedure requires one motion and three announcements, but they do it with four announcements and no motion, it’s an illegitimate legal procedure.

pli-tv-kd9:3.5.1.eng: And what’s a legal procedure done by an incomplete assembly?

pli-tv-kd9:3.5.2.pli: Ñattidutiye ce, bhikkhave, kamme yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā te anāgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando anāhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā paṭikkosanti—vaggakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.5.2.eng: When a procedure requires one motion and one announcement, but the monks who should take part haven’t all arrived, and the consent hasn’t been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and someone present objects to the decision, then it’s a legal procedure done by an incomplete assembly.
pli-tv-kd9:3.5.2.comm: Sp 3.388: Kammappattoti kammaṁ patto, kammayutto kammāraho; na kiñci kammaṁ kātuṁ nārahatīti attho, “‘Who should be present’: who are able in regard to the legal procedure, suitable for the legal procedure, fit for the legal procedure. The meaning is that one should not not do any kind of legal procedure.” The last line means one should or must be present at the legal procedure.

pli-tv-kd9:3.5.3.pli: Ñattidutiye ce, bhikkhave, kamme yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā te āgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando anāhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā paṭikkosanti—vaggakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.5.3.eng: When a procedure requires one motion and one announcement, and the monks who should take part have arrived, but the consent hasn’t been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and someone present objects to the decision, then it’s a legal procedure done by an incomplete assembly.

pli-tv-kd9:3.5.4.pli: Ñattidutiye ce, bhikkhave, kamme yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā te āgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando āhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā paṭikkosanti—vaggakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.5.4.eng: When a procedure requires one motion and one announcement, and the monks who should take part have arrived, and consent has been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, but someone present objects to the decision, then it’s a legal procedure done by an incomplete assembly.

pli-tv-kd9:3.5.5.pli: Ñatticatutthe ce, bhikkhave, kamme yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā te anāgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando anāhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā paṭikkosanti—vaggakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.5.5.eng: When a procedure requires one motion and three announcements, but the monks who should take part haven’t all arrived, and consent hasn’t been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and someone present objects to the decision, then it’s a legal procedure done by an incomplete assembly.

pli-tv-kd9:3.5.6.pli: Ñatticatutthe ce, bhikkhave, kamme yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā te āgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando anāhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā paṭikkosanti—vaggakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.5.6.eng: When a procedure requires one motion and three announcements, and the monks who should take part have arrived, but consent hasn’t been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and someone present objects to the decision, then it’s a legal procedure done by an incomplete assembly.

pli-tv-kd9:3.5.7.pli: Ñatticatutthe ce, bhikkhave, kamme yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā te āgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando āhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā paṭikkosanti—vaggakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.5.7.eng: When a procedure requires one motion and three announcements, and the monks who should take part have arrived, and consent has been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, but someone present objects to the decision, then it’s a legal procedure done by an incomplete assembly.

pli-tv-kd9:3.6.1.eng: And what’s a legal procedure done by a unanimous assembly?

pli-tv-kd9:3.6.2.pli: Ñattidutiye ce, bhikkhave, kamme yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā, te āgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando āhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā na paṭikkosanti—samaggakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.6.2.eng: When a procedure requires one motion and one announcement, and the monks who should take part have arrived, and consent has been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and no-one present objects to the decision, then it’s a legal procedure done by a unanimous assembly.

pli-tv-kd9:3.6.3.pli: Ñatticatutthe ce, bhikkhave, kamme yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā, te āgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando āhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā na paṭikkosanti—samaggakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.6.3.eng: When a procedure requires one motion and three announcements, and the monks who should take part have arrived, and consent has been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and no-one present objects to the decision, then it’s a legal procedure done by a unanimous assembly.

pli-tv-kd9:3.7.1.eng: And what’s a legitimate-like legal procedure done by an incomplete assembly?

pli-tv-kd9:3.7.2.pli: Ñattidutiye ce, bhikkhave, kamme paṭhamaṁ kammavācaṁ anussāveti, pacchā ñattiṁ ṭhapeti, yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā te anāgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando anāhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā paṭikkosanti—dhammapatirūpakena vaggakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.7.2.eng: When a procedure requires one motion and one announcement, but they make the announcement first and put forward the motion afterwards, and the monks who should take part haven’t all arrived, and consent hasn’t been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and someone present objects to the decision, then it’s a legitimate-like legal procedure done by an incomplete assembly.

pli-tv-kd9:3.7.3.pli: Ñattidutiye ce, bhikkhave, kamme paṭhamaṁ kammavācaṁ anussāveti, pacchā ñattiṁ ṭhapeti, yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā te āgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando anāhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā paṭikkosanti—dhammapatirūpakena vaggakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.7.3.eng: When a procedure requires one motion and one announcement, but they make the announcement first and put forward the motion afterwards, yet the monks who should take part have arrived, but consent hasn’t been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and someone present objects to the decision, then it’s a legitimate-like legal procedure done by an incomplete assembly.

pli-tv-kd9:3.7.4.pli: Ñattidutiye ce, bhikkhave, kamme paṭhamaṁ kammavācaṁ anussāveti, pacchā ñattiṁ ṭhapeti, yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā te āgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando āhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā paṭikkosanti—dhammapatirūpakena vaggakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.7.4.eng: When a procedure requires one motion and one announcement, but they make the announcement first and put forward the motion afterwards, yet the monks who should take part have arrived, and consent has been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, but someone present objects to the decision, then it’s a legitimate-like legal procedure done by an incomplete assembly.

pli-tv-kd9:3.7.5.pli: Ñatticatutthe ce, bhikkhave, kamme paṭhamaṁ kammavācaṁ anussāveti, pacchā ñattiṁ ṭhapeti, yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā te anāgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando anāhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā paṭikkosanti—dhammapatirūpakena vaggakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.7.5.eng: When a procedure requires one motion and three announcements, but they make the announcements first and put forward the motion afterwards, and if the monks who should take part haven’t all arrived, and consent hasn’t been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and someone present objects to the decision, then it’s a legitimate-like legal procedure done by an incomplete assembly.

pli-tv-kd9:3.7.6.pli: Ñatticatutthe ce, bhikkhave, kamme paṭhamaṁ kammavācaṁ anussāveti, pacchā ñattiṁ ṭhapeti, yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā te āgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando anāhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā paṭikkosanti—dhammapatirūpakena vaggakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.7.6.eng: When a procedure requires one motion and three announcements, but they make the announcements first and put forward the motion afterwards, yet the monks who should take part have arrived, but consent hasn’t been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and someone present objects to the decision, then it’s a legitimate-like legal procedure done by an incomplete assembly.

pli-tv-kd9:3.7.7.pli: Ñatticatutthe ce, bhikkhave, kamme paṭhamaṁ kammavācaṁ anussāveti, pacchā ñattiṁ ṭhapeti, yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā te āgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando āhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā paṭikkosanti—dhammapatirūpakena vaggakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.7.7.eng: When a procedure requires one motion and three announcements, but they make the announcements first and put forward the motion afterwards, yet the monks who should take part have arrived, and consent has been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, but someone present objects to the decision, then it’s a legitimate-like legal procedure done by an incomplete assembly.

pli-tv-kd9:3.8.1.eng: And what’s a legitimate-like legal procedure done by a unanimous assembly?

pli-tv-kd9:3.8.2.pli: Ñattidutiye ce, bhikkhave, kamme paṭhamaṁ kammavācaṁ anussāveti, pacchā ñattiṁ ṭhapeti, yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā, te āgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando āhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā na paṭikkosanti—dhammapatirūpakena samaggakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.8.2.eng: When a procedure requires one motion and one announcement, but they make the announcement first and put forward the motion afterwards, yet the monks who should take part have arrived, and consent has been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and no-one present objects to the decision, then it’s a legitimate-like legal procedure done by a unanimous assembly.

pli-tv-kd9:3.8.3.pli: Ñatticatutthe ce, bhikkhave, kamme paṭhamaṁ kammavācaṁ anussāveti, pacchā ñattiṁ ṭhapeti, yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā te āgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando āhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā na paṭikkosanti—dhammapatirūpakena samaggakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.8.3.eng: When a procedure requires one motion and three announcements, but they make the announcements first and put forward the motion afterwards, yet the monks who should take part have arrived, and consent has been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and no-one present objects to the decision, then it’s a legitimate-like legal procedure done by a unanimous assembly.

pli-tv-kd9:3.9.1.eng: And what is a legitimate legal procedure done by a unanimous assembly?

pli-tv-kd9:3.9.2.pli: Ñattidutiye ce, bhikkhave, kamme paṭhamaṁ ñattiṁ ṭhapeti, pacchā ekāya kammavācāya kammaṁ karoti, yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā te āgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando āhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā na paṭikkosanti—dhammena samaggakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.9.2.eng: When a procedure requires one motion and one announcement, and they put forward the motion first and make the announcement afterwards, and the monks who should take part have arrived, and consent has been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and no-one present objects to the decision, then it’s a legitimate legal procedure done by a unanimous assembly.

pli-tv-kd9:3.9.3.pli: Ñatticatutthe ce, bhikkhave, kamme paṭhamaṁ ñattiṁ ṭhapeti, pacchā tīhi kammavācāhi kammaṁ karoti, yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā, te āgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando āhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā na paṭikkosanti, dhammena samaggakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:3.9.3.eng: When a procedure requires one motion and three announcements, and they put forward the motion first and make the announcements afterwards, and the monks who should take part have arrived, and consent has been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and no-one present objects to the decision, then it’s a legitimate legal procedure done by a unanimous assembly.”

pli-tv-kd9:4.1.0.eng: 4. Discussion of what can be done by a group of four, etc.

pli-tv-kd9:4.1.2.pli: catuvaggo bhikkhusaṅgho pañcavaggo bhikkhusaṅgho, dasavaggo bhikkhusaṅgho, vīsativaggo bhikkhusaṅgho, atirekavīsativaggo bhikkhusaṅgho.
pli-tv-kd9:4.1.2.eng: a sangha of monks consisting of a group of four, a sangha of monks consisting of a group of five, a sangha of monks consisting of a group of ten, a sangha of monks consisting of a group of twenty, a sangha of monks consisting of a group of more than twenty.

pli-tv-kd9:4.1.3.pli: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ catuvaggo bhikkhusaṅgho, ṭhapetvā tīṇi kammāni—
pli-tv-kd9:4.1.3.eng: A Sangha of monks consisting of a group of four—unanimous, acting legitimately—is able to do all legal procedures except three:

pli-tv-kd9:4.1.4.pli: upasampadaṁ pavāraṇaṁ abbhānaṁ, dhammena samaggo sabbakammesu kammappatto.

pli-tv-kd9:4.1.5.pli: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ pañcavaggo bhikkhusaṅgho, ṭhapetvā dve kammāni—
pli-tv-kd9:4.1.5.eng: A Sangha of monks consisting of a group of five—unanimous, acting legitimately—is able to do all legal procedures except two:

pli-tv-kd9:4.1.6.pli: majjhimesu janapadesu upasampadaṁ abbhānaṁ, dhammena samaggo sabbakammesu kammappatto.
pli-tv-kd9:4.1.6.eng: ordination within the central Ganges plain and rehabilitation.

pli-tv-kd9:4.1.7.pli: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ dasavaggo bhikkhusaṅgho, ṭhapetvā ekaṁ kammaṁ—
pli-tv-kd9:4.1.7.eng: A Sangha of monks consisting of a group of ten—unanimous, acting legitimately—is able to do all legal procedures except one:

pli-tv-kd9:4.1.9.pli: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ vīsativaggo bhikkhusaṅgho dhammena samaggo sabbakammesu kammappatto.
pli-tv-kd9:4.1.9.eng: A Sangha of monks consisting of a group of twenty—unanimous, acting legitimately—is able to do all legal procedures.

pli-tv-kd9:4.1.10.pli: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ atirekavīsativaggo bhikkhusaṅgho dhammena samaggo sabbakammesu kammappatto.
pli-tv-kd9:4.1.10.eng: A Sangha of monks consisting of a group of more than twenty—unanimous, acting legitimately—is able to do all legal procedures.

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.1.pli: Catuvaggakaraṇañce, bhikkhave, kammaṁ bhikkhunicatuttho kammaṁ kareyya—
pli-tv-kd9:4.2.1.eng: If a legal procedure that requires a group of four is done with a nun as the fourth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.2.eng: it’s invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.3.pli: Catuvaggakaraṇañce, bhikkhave, kammaṁ sikkhamānacatuttho kammaṁ kareyya …
pli-tv-kd9:4.2.3.eng: If a legal procedure that requires a group of four is done with a trainee nun as the fourth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.5.eng: with a novice monk as the fourth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.7.eng: with a novice nun as the fourth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.9.eng: with one who’s renounced the training as the fourth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.11.eng: with one who’s committed the worst kind of offense as the fourth member,
pli-tv-kd9:4.2.11.comm: Sp-yoj 5.483: Antimavatthunti pārājikavatthuṁ; “Antimavatthu: an action that is the basis for an offense entailing expulsion.”

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.13.eng: with one who’s been ejected for not recognizing an offense as the fourth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.15.eng: with one who’s been ejected for not making amends for an offense as the fourth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.17.pli: Pāpikāya diṭṭhiyā appaṭinissagge ukkhittakacatuttho kammaṁ kareyya …
pli-tv-kd9:4.2.17.eng: with one who’s been ejected for not giving up a bad view as the fourth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.19.eng: with a paṇḍaka as the fourth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.21.eng: with a fake monk as the fourth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.23.eng: with one who’s previously left to join the monastics of another religion as the fourth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.25.eng: with an animal as the fourth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.27.eng: with a matricide as the fourth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.29.eng: with a patricide as the fourth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.31.eng: with a murderer of a perfected one as the fourth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.33.eng: with one who’s raped a nun as the fourth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.35.eng: with one who’s caused a schism in the Sangha as the fourth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.37.eng: with one who’s caused the Buddha to bleed as the fourth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.39.eng: with a hermaphrodite as the fourth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.41.eng: with one belonging to a different Buddhist sect as the fourth member,
pli-tv-kd9:4.2.41.comm: Nānāsaṁvāsaka (and samānasaṁvāsaka) need to be carefully distinguished from nānāsaṁvāsa (and samānasaṁvāsa). Only the former means “one belonging to a different Buddhist sect”. The latter means “belonging to a different community”, as decided by sīmās.

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.43.eng: with one who’s outside the monastery zone as the fourth member,
pli-tv-kd9:4.2.43.comm: Nānāsīmāya literally means “within a different monastery zone”. Sp 3.389: Nānāsīmāya ṭhitacatutthoti sīmantarikāya vā bahisīmāya vā hatthapāse ṭhitenāpi saddhiṁ catuvaggo hutvāti attho, “Nānāsīmāya ṭhitacatuttho, the meaning is: having been a group of four, including one who, even if within arm’s reach, is in the space between monastery zones or outside the monastery zone.”

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.45.eng: with one floating in the air by supernormal power as the fourth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.47.pli: Yassa saṅgho kammaṁ karoti, taṁcatuttho kammaṁ kareyya …
pli-tv-kd9:4.2.47.eng: or with one who’s subject to the legal procedure as the fourth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.2.48.eng: it’s invalid and not to be done.”

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.1.eng: Procedures requiring a group of four is finished.

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.2.pli: Pañcavaggakaraṇañce, bhikkhave, kammaṁ bhikkhunipañcamo kammaṁ kareyya …
pli-tv-kd9:4.3.2.eng: “If a legal procedure that requires a group of five is done with a nun as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.3.eng: it’s invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.4.pli: Pañcavaggakaraṇañce, bhikkhave, kammaṁ sikkhamānapañcamo kammaṁ kareyya …pe….
pli-tv-kd9:4.3.4.eng: If a legal procedure that requires a group of five is done with a trainee nun as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.5.eng: with a novice monk as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.6.eng: with a novice nun as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.7.eng: with one who’s renounced the training as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.8.eng: with one who’s committed the worst kind of offense as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.9.eng: with one who’s been ejected for not recognizing an offense as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.10.eng: with one who’s been ejected for not making amends for an offense as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.11.pli: pāpikāya diṭṭhiyā appaṭinissagge ukkhittakapañcamo kammaṁ kareyya …
pli-tv-kd9:4.3.11.eng: with one who’s been ejected for not giving up a bad view as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.12.eng: with a paṇḍaka as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.13.eng: with a fake monk as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.14.eng: with one who’s previously left to join the monastics of another religion as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.15.eng: with an animal as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.16.eng: with a matricide as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.17.eng: with a patricide as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.18.eng: with a murderer of a perfected one as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.19.eng: with one who’s raped a nun as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.20.eng: with one who’s caused a schism in the Sangha as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.21.eng: with one who’s caused the Buddha to bleed as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.22.eng: with a hermaphrodite as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.23.eng: with one belonging to a different Buddhist sect as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.24.eng: with one who’s outside the monastery zone as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.25.eng: with one floating in the air by supernormal power as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.26.pli: yassa saṅgho kammaṁ karoti, taṁpañcamo kammaṁ kareyya—
pli-tv-kd9:4.3.26.eng: or with one who’s subject to the legal procedure as the fifth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.3.27.eng: it’s invalid and not to be done.”

pli-tv-kd9:4.4.1.eng: Procedures requiring a group of five is finished.

pli-tv-kd9:4.4.2.pli: Dasavaggakaraṇañce, bhikkhave, kammaṁ bhikkhunidasamo kammaṁ kareyya, akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:4.4.2.eng: “If a legal procedure that requires a group of ten is done with a nun as the tenth member, it’s invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:4.4.3.pli: Dasavaggakaraṇañce, bhikkhave, kammaṁ sikkhamānadasamo kammaṁ kareyya, akammaṁ na ca karaṇīyaṁ …
pli-tv-kd9:4.4.3.eng: If a legal procedure that requires a group of ten is done with a trainee nun as the tenth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.4.4.eng: with a novice monk as the tenth member, with a novice nun as the tenth member, with one who’s renounced the training as the tenth member, with one who’s committed the worst kind of offense as the tenth member, with one who’s been ejected for not recognizing an offense as the tenth member, with one who’s been ejected for not making amends for an offense as the tenth member, with one who’s been ejected for not giving up a bad view as the tenth member, with a paṇḍaka as the tenth member, with one living in the community by theft as the tenth member, with one who’s previously left to join the monastics of another religion as the tenth member, with an animal as the tenth member, with a matricide as the tenth member, with a patricide as the tenth member, with a murderer of a perfected one as the tenth member, with one who’s raped a nun as the tenth member, with one who’s caused a schism in the Sangha as the tenth member, with one who’s caused the Buddha to bleed as the tenth member, with a hermaphrodite as the tenth member, with one belonging to a different Buddhist sect as the tenth member, with one who’s outside the monastery zone as the tenth member, with one floating in the air by supernormal power as the tenth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.4.5.pli: Dasavaggakaraṇañce, bhikkhave, kammaṁ yassa saṅgho kammaṁ karoti, taṁdasamo kammaṁ kareyya—
pli-tv-kd9:4.4.5.eng: or with one who’s subject to the legal procedure as the tenth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.4.6.eng: it’s invalid and not to be done.”

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.1.eng: Procedures requiring a group of ten is finished.

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.2.pli: Vīsativaggakaraṇañce, bhikkhave, kammaṁ bhikkhunivīso kammaṁ kareyya—
pli-tv-kd9:4.5.2.eng: “If a legal procedure that requires a group of twenty is done with a nun as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.3.eng: it’s invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.4.pli: Vīsativaggakaraṇañce, bhikkhave, kammaṁ sikkhamānavīso kammaṁ kareyya …pe…
pli-tv-kd9:4.5.4.eng: If a legal procedure that requires a group of twenty is done with a trainee nun as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.5.eng: with a novice monk as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.6.eng: with a novice nun as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.7.eng: with one who’s renounced the training as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.8.eng: with one who’s committed the worst kind of offense as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.9.eng: with one who’s been ejected for not recognizing an offense as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.10.eng: with one who’s been ejected for not making amends for an offense as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.11.pli: pāpikāya diṭṭhiyā appaṭinissagge ukkhittakavīso kammaṁ kareyya …
pli-tv-kd9:4.5.11.eng: with one who’s been ejected for not giving up a bad view as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.12.eng: with a paṇḍaka as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.13.eng: with a fake monk as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.14.eng: with one who’s previously left to join the monastics of another religion as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.15.eng: with an animal as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.16.eng: with a matricide as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.17.eng: with a patricide as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.18.eng: with a murderer of a perfected one as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.19.eng: with one who’s raped a nun as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.20.eng: with one who’s caused a schism in the Sangha as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.21.eng: with one who’s caused the Buddha to bleed as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.22.eng: with a hermaphrodite as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.23.eng: with one belonging to a different Buddhist sect as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.24.eng: with one who’s outside the monastery zone as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.25.eng: with one floating in the air by supernormal power as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.26.pli: yassa saṅgho kammaṁ karoti, taṁvīso kammaṁ kareyya—
pli-tv-kd9:4.5.26.eng: or with one who’s subject to the legal procedure as the twentieth member,

pli-tv-kd9:4.5.27.eng: it’s invalid and not to be done.”

pli-tv-kd9:4.6.1.eng: Procedures requiring a group of twenty is finished.

pli-tv-kd9:4.6.2.0.eng: 5. Discussion of the one on probation, etc.

pli-tv-kd9:4.6.2.1.pli: Pārivāsikacatuttho ce, bhikkhave, parivāsaṁ dadeyya, mūlāya paṭikasseyya, mānattaṁ dadeyya, taṁvīso abbheyya—
pli-tv-kd9:4.6.2.1.eng: “If a group with one on probation as the fourth member gives probation, sends back to the beginning, or gives the trial period, or a group with one on probation as the twentieth member rehabilitates,

pli-tv-kd9:4.6.3.eng: it’s invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:4.6.4.pli: Mūlāyapaṭikassanārahacatuttho ce, bhikkhave, parivāsaṁ dadeyya, mūlāya paṭikasseyya, mānattaṁ dadeyya, taṁvīso abbheyya—
pli-tv-kd9:4.6.4.eng: If a group with one deserving to be sent back to the beginning as the fourth member gives probation, sends back to the beginning, or gives the trial period, or a group with one deserving to be sent back to the beginning as the twentieth member rehabilitates,

pli-tv-kd9:4.6.5.eng: it’s invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:4.6.6.pli: Mānattārahacatuttho ce, bhikkhave, parivāsaṁ dadeyya, mūlāya paṭikasseyya, mānattaṁ dadeyya, taṁvīso abbheyya—
pli-tv-kd9:4.6.6.eng: If a group with one deserving the trial period as the fourth member gives probation, sends back to the beginning, or gives the trial period, or a group with one deserving a trial period as the twentieth member rehabilitates,

pli-tv-kd9:4.6.7.eng: it’s invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:4.6.8.pli: Mānattacārikacatuttho ce, bhikkhave, parivāsaṁ dadeyya, mūlāya paṭikasseyya, mānattaṁ dadeyya, taṁvīso abbheyya—
pli-tv-kd9:4.6.8.eng: If a group with one undertaking the trial period as the fourth member gives probation, sends back to the beginning, or gives the trial period, or a group with one undertaking a trial period as the twentieth member rehabilitates,

pli-tv-kd9:4.6.9.eng: it’s invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:4.6.10.pli: Abbhānārahacatuttho ce, bhikkhave, parivāsaṁ dadeyya, mūlāya paṭikasseyya, mānattaṁ dadeyya, taṁvīso abbheyya—
pli-tv-kd9:4.6.10.eng: If a group with one deserving rehabilitation as the fourth member gives probation, sends back to the beginning, or gives the trial period, or a group with one deserving rehabilitation as the twentieth member rehabilitates,

pli-tv-kd9:4.6.11.eng: it’s invalid and not to be done.

pli-tv-kd9:4.7.1.pli: Ekaccassa, bhikkhave, saṅghamajjhe paṭikkosanā ruhati, ekaccassa na ruhati.
pli-tv-kd9:4.7.1.eng: In the midst of the Sangha, the objections of some are valid, not the objections of others.

pli-tv-kd9:4.7.2.pli: Kassa ca, bhikkhave, saṅghamajjhe paṭikkosanā na ruhati?
pli-tv-kd9:4.7.2.eng: Whose objections are invalid in the midst of the Sangha?

pli-tv-kd9:4.7.3.pli: Bhikkhuniyā, bhikkhave, saṅghamajjhe paṭikkosanā na ruhati.
pli-tv-kd9:4.7.3.eng: In the midst of the Sangha, the objection of a nun is invalid.

pli-tv-kd9:4.7.4.eng: In the midst of the Sangha, the objection of a trainee nun,

pli-tv-kd9:4.7.7.eng: of one who’s renounced the training,

pli-tv-kd9:4.7.8.eng: of one who’s committed the worst kind of offense,

pli-tv-kd9:4.7.11.eng: of one who’s overwhelmed by pain,

pli-tv-kd9:4.7.12.eng: of one who’s been ejected for not recognizing an offense,

pli-tv-kd9:4.7.13.eng: of one who’s been ejected for not making amends for an offense,

pli-tv-kd9:4.7.14.eng: of one who’s been ejected for not giving up a bad view,

pli-tv-kd9:4.7.17.eng: of one who’s previously left to join the monastics of another religion,

pli-tv-kd9:4.7.21.eng: of a murderer of a perfected one,

pli-tv-kd9:4.7.22.eng: of one who’s raped a nun,

pli-tv-kd9:4.7.23.eng: of one who’s caused a schism in the Sangha,

pli-tv-kd9:4.7.24.eng: of one who’s caused the Buddha to bleed,

pli-tv-kd9:4.7.26.eng: of one who belongs to a different Buddhist sect,

pli-tv-kd9:4.7.27.eng: of one who’s outside the monastery zone,

pli-tv-kd9:4.7.28.pli: iddhiyā vehāse ṭhitassa, bhikkhave, yassa saṅgho kammaṁ karoti, tassa ca, bhikkhave, saṅghamajjhe paṭikkosanā na ruhati.
pli-tv-kd9:4.7.28.eng: of one floating in the air by supernormal power,

pli-tv-kd9:4.7.29.pli: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, saṅghamajjhe paṭikkosanā na ruhati.
pli-tv-kd9:4.7.29.eng: or of one who’s subject to the legal procedure is invalid.

pli-tv-kd9:4.8.1.pli: Kassa ca, bhikkhave, saṅghamajjhe paṭikkosanā ruhati?
pli-tv-kd9:4.8.1.eng: And whose objections are valid in the midst of the Sangha?

pli-tv-kd9:4.8.2.pli: Bhikkhussa, bhikkhave, pakatattassa samānasaṁvāsakassa samānasīmāya ṭhitassa antamaso ānantarikassāpi bhikkhuno viññāpentassa saṅghamajjhe paṭikkosanā ruhati.
pli-tv-kd9:4.8.2.eng: In the midst of the Sangha, the objection of a regular monk, who belongs to the same Buddhist sect and is staying within the same monastery zone, even if just declared to a monk sitting next to him, is valid.

pli-tv-kd9:4.9.0.eng: 6. Discussion of the two kinds of sending away, etc.

pli-tv-kd9:4.9.1.eng: “There are two kinds of sending away.

pli-tv-kd9:4.9.2.eng: If the Sangha sends away someone who doesn’t have the attributes needed to be sent away,

pli-tv-kd9:4.9.3.pli: Tañce saṅgho nissāreti, ekacco sunissārito, ekacco dunnissārito.
pli-tv-kd9:4.9.3.eng: the sending away may succeed or fail.

pli-tv-kd9:4.9.4.pli: Katamo ca, bhikkhave, puggalo appatto nissāraṇaṁ, tañce saṅgho nissāreti—dunnissārito?

pli-tv-kd9:4.9.5.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu suddho hoti anāpattiko. Tañce saṅgho nissāreti—dunnissārito.
pli-tv-kd9:4.9.5.eng: It fails if the monk is pure, without offenses.

pli-tv-kd9:4.9.6.pli: Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, puggalo appatto nissāraṇaṁ, tañce saṅgho nissāreti—dunnissārito.

pli-tv-kd9:4.9.7.pli: Katamo ca, bhikkhave, puggalo appatto nissāraṇaṁ, tañce saṅgho nissāreti—sunissārito?

pli-tv-kd9:4.9.8.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu bālo hoti abyatto āpattibahulo anapadāno, gihisaṁsaṭṭho viharati ananulomikehi gihisaṁsaggehi, tañce saṅgho nissāreti—sunissārito.
pli-tv-kd9:4.9.8.eng: It succeeds if the monk is ignorant, incompetent, often committing offenses, lacking in boundaries, constantly and improperly socializing with householders.
pli-tv-kd9:4.9.8.comm: According to CPD, apparently quoting the commentary (“Bu”), anapadāna means “‘who is unable to discern (what is an offense)’, or ‘not setting a good example’.” It is not clear, however, why apadāna should be rendered as “discern”. Sp 3.407: Apadānaṁ vuccati paricchedo; āpattiparicchedavirahitoti attho, “Limit is called apadāna; the meaning is ‘without limit to offenses’.” Sp-ṭ 3.395: Natthi etassa apadānaṁ avakhaṇḍanaṁ āpattipariyantoti anapadāno, “Anapadāno: he has no apadāna, no cutting off, no limit with offenses.”

pli-tv-kd9:4.9.9.pli: Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, puggalo appatto nissāraṇaṁ, tañce saṅgho nissāreti—sunissārito.

pli-tv-kd9:4.10.1.eng: There are two kinds of admittance.

pli-tv-kd9:4.10.2.pli: Atthi, bhikkhave, puggalo appatto osāraṇaṁ tañce saṅgho osāreti, ekacco sosārito, ekacco dosārito.
pli-tv-kd9:4.10.2.eng: If the Sangha admits someone who doesn’t have the attributes needed to be admitted, the admittance may succeed or fail.

pli-tv-kd9:4.10.3.pli: Katamo ca, bhikkhave, puggalo appatto osāraṇaṁ, tañce saṅgho osāreti—dosārito?

pli-tv-kd9:4.10.4.pli: Paṇḍako, bhikkhave, appatto osāraṇaṁ, tañce saṅgho osāreti—dosārito.
pli-tv-kd9:4.10.4.eng: A paṇḍaka doesn’t have the attributes needed to be admitted, and if the Sangha admits him, his admittance fails.

pli-tv-kd9:4.10.5.pli: Theyyasaṁvāsako, bhikkhave, appatto osāraṇaṁ, tañce saṅgho osāreti—dosārito.

pli-tv-kd9:4.10.6.eng: one who’s previously left to join the monastics of another religion,

pli-tv-kd9:4.10.10.eng: a murderer of a perfected one,

pli-tv-kd9:4.10.12.eng: one who’s caused a schism in the Sangha,

pli-tv-kd9:4.10.13.eng: one who’s caused the Buddha to bleed,

pli-tv-kd9:4.10.14.pli: ubhatobyañjanako, bhikkhave, appatto, osāraṇaṁ, tañce saṅgho osāreti—dosārito.
pli-tv-kd9:4.10.14.eng: or a hermaphrodite doesn’t have the attributes needed to be admitted, and if the Sangha admits him, his admittance fails.

pli-tv-kd9:4.10.15.pli: Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, puggalo appatto osāraṇaṁ, tañce saṅgho osāreti—dosārito.

pli-tv-kd9:4.10.16.pli: Ime vuccanti, bhikkhave, puggalā appattā osāraṇaṁ, te ce saṅgho osāreti—dosāritā.

pli-tv-kd9:4.11.1.pli: Katamo ca, bhikkhave, puggalo appatto osāraṇaṁ, tañce saṅgho osāreti—sosārito?

pli-tv-kd9:4.11.2.pli: Hatthacchinno, bhikkhave, appatto osāraṇaṁ, tañce saṅgho osāreti, sosārito.
pli-tv-kd9:4.11.2.eng: One without a hand doesn’t have the attributes needed to be admitted, but if the Sangha admits him, his admittance succeeds.

pli-tv-kd9:4.11.4.eng: one without a hand and a foot,

pli-tv-kd9:4.11.7.eng: one without an ear and nose,

pli-tv-kd9:4.11.8.eng: one without a finger or toe,
pli-tv-kd9:4.11.8.comm: This single phrase combines two Pali terms, aṅgulicchinna and aḷacchinna. The latter refers to a thumb or a big toe, whereas the former refers to any of the remaining four fingers or toes.

pli-tv-kd9:4.11.11.comm: Phaṇahatthaka, literally, “one who has a hand like a snake’s hood”. Sp 3.119: Phaṇahatthakoti yassa vaggulipakkhakā viya aṅguliyo sambaddhā honti, “Phaṇahatthako: one whose fingers are connected like the wings of a bat.”

pli-tv-kd9:4.11.17.comm: Likhitaka, literally, “one who has been written about”. Sp 3.119: Atha kho yo koci corikaṁ vā aññaṁ vā garuṁ rājāparādhaṁ katvā palāto, rājā ca naṁ paṇṇe vā potthake vā “itthannāmo yattha dissati, tattha gahetvā māretabbo”ti vā “hatthapādānissa chinditabbānī”ti vā “ettakaṁ nāma daṇḍaṁ āharāpetabbo”ti vā likhāpeti, ayaṁ likhitako nāma, “When someone has run away after stealing or doing another serious offense against the king, and the king causes the writing about him on a leaf or in a book that ‘wherever so-and-so is seen, he should be seized and executed’ or ‘his hands and feet are to be cut off’ or ‘this penalty is to be imposed’, this a called a wanted criminal.”

pli-tv-kd9:4.11.20.comm: Parisadūsaka, literally, “one who defiles an assembly”. Sp 3.93: Parisadūsakoti yo attano virūpatāya parisaṁ dūseti; atidīgho vā hoti aññesaṁ sīsappamāṇanābhippadeso, atirasso vā …, “Parisadūsaka: whoever defiles an assembly through his own bad appearance. He is too tall, a head taller than others, or he is too short …”

pli-tv-kd9:4.11.24.comm: Sp 3.119: Pakkhahatoti yassa eko hattho vā pādo vā aḍḍhasarīraṁ vā sukhaṁ na vahati, “Pakkhahata: for whom one hand or one foot or half the body does not work properly.”

pli-tv-kd9:4.11.25.comm: Chinniriyāpatha, literally, “the ways of movement have been cut off”. Sp 3.119: Chinniriyāpathoti pīṭhasappi vuccati, “One who crawls is called chinniriyāpatha.” The exact meaning is not clear.

pli-tv-kd9:4.11.33.pli: andhamūgabadhiro, bhikkhave, appatto osāraṇaṁ, tañce saṅgho osāreti—sosārito.
pli-tv-kd9:4.11.33.eng: or one who’s blind and mute and deaf doesn’t have the attributes needed to be admitted, but if the Sangha admits him, his admittance succeeds.”

pli-tv-kd9:4.11.34.pli: Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, puggalo appatto osāraṇaṁ, tañce saṅgho osāreti—sosārito.

pli-tv-kd9:4.11.35.pli: Ime vuccanti, bhikkhave, puggalā appattā osāraṇaṁ, te ce saṅgho osāreti—sosāritā.

pli-tv-kd9:4.11.36.eng: The first section for recitation on the village of Vāsabha is finished.

pli-tv-kd9:5.1.0.eng: 7. Discussion of illegitimate legal procedures, etc.

pli-tv-kd9:5.1.1.1.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhussa na hoti āpatti daṭṭhabbā.
pli-tv-kd9:5.1.1.1.eng: “It may be that a monk doesn’t have any offense he needs to recognize,

pli-tv-kd9:5.1.2.pli: Tamenaṁ codeti saṅgho vā sambahulā vā ekapuggalo vā—
pli-tv-kd9:5.1.2.eng: yet a Sangha, several monks, or an individual monk accuses him, saying:
pli-tv-kd9:5.1.2.comm: “Several” renders sambahula. See Appendix of Technical Terms.

pli-tv-kd9:5.1.3.eng: ‘You’ve committed an offense. Do you recognize it?’

pli-tv-kd9:5.1.5.eng: ‘I haven’t committed any offense that I should recognize,’

pli-tv-kd9:5.1.6.eng: yet the Sangha ejects him for not recognizing an offense,

pli-tv-kd9:5.1.7.eng: then the legal procedure is illegitimate.

pli-tv-kd9:5.1.8.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhussa na hoti āpatti paṭikātabbā.
pli-tv-kd9:5.1.8.eng: It may be that a monk doesn’t have any offense he needs to make amends for,

pli-tv-kd9:5.1.9.pli: Tamenaṁ codeti saṅgho vā sambahulā vā ekapuggalo vā—
pli-tv-kd9:5.1.9.eng: yet a Sangha, several monks, or an individual monk accuses him, saying:

pli-tv-kd9:5.1.10.pli: ‘āpattiṁ tvaṁ, āvuso, āpanno, paṭikarohi taṁ āpattin’ti.
pli-tv-kd9:5.1.10.eng: ‘You’ve committed an offense. Make amends for it.’

pli-tv-kd9:5.1.12.eng: ‘I haven’t committed any offense that I should make amends for,’

pli-tv-kd9:5.1.13.eng: yet the Sangha ejects him for not making amends for an offense,

pli-tv-kd9:5.1.14.eng: then the legal procedure is illegitimate.

pli-tv-kd9:5.1.15.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhussa na hoti pāpikā diṭṭhi paṭinissajjetā.
pli-tv-kd9:5.1.15.eng: It may be that a monk doesn’t have any bad view he needs to give up,

pli-tv-kd9:5.1.16.pli: Tamenaṁ codeti saṅgho vā sambahulā vā ekapuggalo vā—
pli-tv-kd9:5.1.16.eng: yet a Sangha, several monks, or an individual monk accuses him, saying:

pli-tv-kd9:5.1.17.pli: ‘pāpikā te, āvuso, diṭṭhi, paṭinissajjetaṁ pāpikaṁ diṭṭhin’ti.
pli-tv-kd9:5.1.17.eng: ‘You have a bad view that you need to give up.’

pli-tv-kd9:5.1.19.pli: ‘natthi me, āvuso, pāpikā diṭṭhi, yamahaṁ paṭinissajjeyyan’ti.
pli-tv-kd9:5.1.19.eng: ‘I don’t have any bad view that I should give up,’

pli-tv-kd9:5.1.20.pli: Taṁ saṅgho pāpikāya diṭṭhiyā appaṭinissagge ukkhipati—
pli-tv-kd9:5.1.20.eng: yet the Sangha ejects him for not giving up a bad view,

pli-tv-kd9:5.1.21.eng: then the legal procedure is illegitimate.

pli-tv-kd9:5.2.1.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhussa na hoti āpatti daṭṭhabbā, na hoti āpatti paṭikātabbā.
pli-tv-kd9:5.2.1.eng: It may be that a monk doesn’t have any offense he needs to recognize, nor any he needs to make amends for,

pli-tv-kd9:5.2.2.pli: Tamenaṁ codeti saṅgho vā sambahulā vā ekapuggalo vā—
pli-tv-kd9:5.2.2.eng: yet a Sangha, several monks, or an individual monk accuses him, saying:

pli-tv-kd9:5.2.3.eng: ‘You’ve committed an offense. Do you recognize it?

pli-tv-kd9:5.2.6.eng: ‘I haven’t committed any offense that I should recognize,

pli-tv-kd9:5.2.7.pli: Natthi me, āvuso, āpatti, yamahaṁ paṭikareyyan’ti.
pli-tv-kd9:5.2.7.eng: nor any I should make amends for,’

pli-tv-kd9:5.2.8.pli: Taṁ saṅgho adassane vā appaṭikamme vā ukkhipati—

pli-tv-kd9:6.1.1.pli: Atha kho āyasmā upāli yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami, upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.
pli-tv-kd9:6.1.1.eng: On one occasion Venerable Upāli went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down,

pli-tv-kd9:6.1.2.pli: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā upāli bhagavantaṁ etadavoca—

pli-tv-kd9:6.1.3.pli: “yo nu kho, bhante, samaggo saṅgho sammukhākaraṇīyaṁ kammaṁ asammukhā karoti, dhammakammaṁ nu kho taṁ, bhante, vinayakamman”ti?
pli-tv-kd9:6.1.3.eng: “If, sir, a unanimous Sangha doesn’t do a legal procedure face-to-face that should be done face-to-face, is that a legitimate procedure, in accordance with the Monastic Law?”

pli-tv-kd9:6.1.4.eng: “That legal procedure, Upāli, is illegitimate, contrary to the Monastic Law.”

pli-tv-kd9:6.2.1.pli: “Yo nu kho, bhante, samaggo saṅgho paṭipucchākaraṇīyaṁ kammaṁ appaṭipucchā karoti …pe…
pli-tv-kd9:6.2.1.eng: “If a unanimous Sangha does a procedure without questioning that should be done with questioning,

pli-tv-kd9:6.2.2.eng: does a procedure without admission that should be done with admission,

pli-tv-kd9:6.2.3.eng: applies resolution because of past insanity to one deserving resolution through recollection,

pli-tv-kd9:6.2.4.eng: does a procedure of further penalty against one deserving resolution because of past insanity,

pli-tv-kd9:6.2.5.eng: does a procedure of condemnation against one deserving a procedure of further penalty,

pli-tv-kd9:6.2.6.eng: does a procedure of demotion against one deserving a procedure of condemnation,
pli-tv-kd9:6.2.6.comm: “Demotion” renders niyassa. See Appendix of Technical Terms.

pli-tv-kd9:6.2.7.eng: does a procedure of banishment against one deserving a procedure of demotion,

pli-tv-kd9:6.2.8.eng: does a procedure of reconciliation against one deserving a procedure of banishment,

pli-tv-kd9:6.2.9.eng: does a procedure of ejection against one deserving a procedure of reconciliation,

pli-tv-kd9:6.2.10.eng: gives probation to one deserving a procedure of ejection,

pli-tv-kd9:6.2.11.eng: sends back to the beginning one deserving probation,

pli-tv-kd9:6.2.12.eng: gives the trial period to one deserving to be sent back to the beginning,

pli-tv-kd9:6.2.13.eng: rehabilitates one deserving the trial period,

pli-tv-kd9:6.2.14.pli: abbhānārahaṁ upasampādeti, dhammakammaṁ nu kho taṁ, bhante, vinayakamman”ti?
pli-tv-kd9:6.2.14.eng: or gives full ordination to one deserving rehabilitation, is that a legitimate procedure, in accordance with the Monastic Law?”

pli-tv-kd9:6.3.1.eng: “That legal procedure, Upāli, is illegitimate, contrary to the Monastic Law.

pli-tv-kd9:6.3.2.pli: Yo kho, upāli, samaggo saṅgho sammukhākaraṇīyaṁ kammaṁ asammukhā karoti, evaṁ kho, upāli, adhammakammaṁ hoti avinayakammaṁ, evañca pana saṅgho sātisāro hoti.
pli-tv-kd9:6.3.2.eng: If a unanimous Sangha doesn’t do a legal procedure face-to-face that should be done face-to-face, that procedure is illegitimate, contrary to the Monastic Law, and the Sangha is at fault.

pli-tv-kd9:6.3.3.pli: Yo kho, upāli, samaggo saṅgho paṭipucchākaraṇīyaṁ kammaṁ appaṭipucchā karoti …pe…
pli-tv-kd9:6.3.3.eng: If a unanimous Sangha does a legal procedure without questioning that should be done with questioning,

pli-tv-kd9:6.3.4.eng: does a legal procedure without admission that should be done with admission,

pli-tv-kd9:6.3.5.eng: applies resolution because of past insanity to one deserving resolution through recollection,

pli-tv-kd9:6.3.6.eng: does a legal procedure of further penalty against one deserving resolution because of past insanity,

pli-tv-kd9:6.3.7.eng: does a legal procedure of condemnation against one deserving a procedure of further penalty,

pli-tv-kd9:6.3.8.eng: does a legal procedure of demotion against one deserving a procedure of condemnation,

pli-tv-kd9:6.3.9.eng: does a legal procedure of banishment against one deserving a procedure of demotion,

pli-tv-kd9:6.3.10.eng: does a legal procedure of reconciliation against one deserving a procedure of banishment,

pli-tv-kd9:6.3.11.eng: does a legal procedure of ejection against one deserving a procedure of reconciliation,

pli-tv-kd9:6.3.12.eng: gives probation to one deserving a procedure of ejection,

pli-tv-kd9:6.3.13.eng: sends back to the beginning one deserving probation,

pli-tv-kd9:6.3.14.eng: gives the trial period to one deserving to be sent back to the beginning,

pli-tv-kd9:6.3.15.eng: rehabilitates one deserving the trial period,

pli-tv-kd9:6.3.16.pli: abbhānārahaṁ upasampādeti, evaṁ kho, upāli, adhammakammaṁ hoti avinayakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:6.3.16.eng: or gives full ordination to one deserving rehabilitation, that procedure is illegitimate, contrary to the Monastic Law,

pli-tv-kd9:6.3.17.eng: and the Sangha is at fault.”

pli-tv-kd9:6.4.1.pli: “Yo nu kho, bhante, samaggo saṅgho sammukhākaraṇīyaṁ kammaṁ sammukhā karoti, dhammakammaṁ nu kho taṁ, bhante, vinayakamman”ti?
pli-tv-kd9:6.4.1.eng: “But if, sir, a unanimous Sangha does a legal procedure face-to-face that should be done face-to-face, is that a legitimate procedure, in accordance with the Monastic Law?”

pli-tv-kd9:6.4.3.eng: “If a unanimous Sangha does a procedure with questioning that should be done with questioning,

pli-tv-kd9:6.5.1.pli: “Yo nu kho, bhante, samaggo saṅgho sativinayārahassa amūḷhavinayaṁ deti, amūḷhavinayārahassa sativinayaṁ deti, dhammakammaṁ nu kho taṁ, bhante, vinayakamman”ti?
pli-tv-kd9:6.5.1.eng: “If, sir, a unanimous Sangha applies resolution because of past insanity to one deserving resolution through recollection and applies resolution through recollection to one deserving resolution because of past insanity, is that a legitimate procedure, in accordance with the Monastic Law?”
pli-tv-kd9:6.5.1.comm: The text is not clear about the relationship between the two applications of resolution, but usually an “and” is the default conjunction if nothing else is specified in the text. This seems to be confirmed by the commentary. Sp 3.400: Dvimūlake yathā sativinayo amūḷhavinayena saddhiṁ ekā pucchā katā, “When there are two items, as with resolution through recollection together with resolution through past insanity, a single question is posed.” In other words, the saṅghakamma seems to be against two individuals together.

pli-tv-kd9:6.5.2.eng: “That legal procedure, Upāli, is illegitimate, contrary to the Monastic Law.”

pli-tv-kd9:6.5.3.pli: “Yo nu kho, bhante, samaggo saṅgho amūḷhavinayārahassa tassapāpiyasikākammaṁ karoti, tassapāpiyasikākammārahassa amūḷhavinayaṁ deti …pe…
pli-tv-kd9:6.5.3.eng: “If a unanimous Sangha does a procedure of further penalty against one deserving resolution because of past insanity and applies resolution because of past insanity to one deserving a procedure of further penalty,

pli-tv-kd9:6.9.52.pli: upasampadārahaṁ abbheti, evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, adhammakammaṁ hoti avinayakammaṁ.
pli-tv-kd9:6.9.52.eng: or rehabilitates one deserving full ordination, that procedure is illegitimate, contrary to the Monastic Law,

pli-tv-kd9:6.9.53.eng: and the Sangha is at fault.”

pli-tv-kd9:6.9.54.eng: The second section for recitation on Upāli’s questions is finished.
